discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape led by semis (SMH +4.85%, XLK +3.28%) with QQQ outperforming SPY meaningfully; mega-cap tech and AI infrastructure names bid up while financials, healthcare, and utilities lag. No mid-cap movers were surfaced in the discovery feed, so commentary focuses on macro context and one notable catalyst chain around AI/memory and tariffs.

notable_index_moves: SMH +4.85% and XLK +3.28% vs SPY +0.84% — narrow leadership in semis/AI
AVGO +4.48%, NVDA +2.0%, TSLA +3.76% leading; META -1.09% and MSFT -0.81% lagging mega-cap
XLF -0.50%, XLV -0.80%, XLU -0.48% — defensives and financials soft despite risk-on equity tape
VIX +1.52% to 17.34 — not confirming risk-on, mild hedging into Trump EU tariff headline
TSM -1.49% diverging negatively from SMH — worth noting; possibly tariff/Taiwan-specific

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Nintendo hikes Switch 2 price citing memory crunch; Roundhill Memory ETF (DRAM) added $1B in a day

secondary_opportunities.relationship: primary beneficiary of memory pricing tightness (DRAM/NAND)
NAND/HDD beneficiary of memory shortage narrative
storage peer caught in memory/storage bid

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Trump sets July 4 deadline for EU to ratify trade deal or face 'much higher' tariffs

secondary_opportunities.relationship: global trade/tariff sensitivity; diverging -1.49% vs SMH +4.85% today

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Akamai +20% on earnings and $1.8B AI infrastructure deal

secondary_opportunities.relationship: CDN/edge competitor — sympathy possible but business mix differs
edge/security peer, larger cap, often moves on AKAM read-throughs

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump gave EU until July 4 to ratify trade deal and cut tariffs to zero, or face much higher tariffs. Framed as continuation of Turnberry agreement.
trump_signals.note: Mid-cap US implications are second-order: US auto parts (e.g., MGA, LEA, APTV), industrials with European exposure, and luxury importers. Market response so far is muted (SPY +0.84%, VIX only +1.52%), suggesting traders are treating this as negotiating posture rather than a hard deadline. The Toyota -49% profit slump headline from earlier today provides a real-world read-through that tariffs are biting global autos — worth watching mid-cap auto suppliers for delayed reactions.

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump met with Brazil's Lula on trade/tariffs, described as going 'very well' with follow-up meetings scheduled.
trump_signals.note: Mildly constructive tone for Brazil-exposed names. Embraer (ERJ) is the most direct mid-cap beneficiary of any tariff softening — worth watching for follow-through.

discovery.no_signals_note: Discovery feed was empty — no mid-cap movers surfaced today despite a sharp tech/semi rally. This is unusual on a +3.28% XLK day and may reflect a data gap rather than genuine quiet at the mid-cap level. Michael should treat the absence of named discoveries as 'I cannot see the mid-cap tape today' rather than 'nothing is happening.' The catalyst chains above are derived from news flow, not from observed price moves.

ai_announcements.headline: OpenAI launches new voice intelligence features in its API
ai_announcements.summary: OpenAI added voice intelligence capabilities to its API, targeting customer service, education, and creator platforms.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: NICE CXone already integrates LLM voice; enterprises need compliance, routing, workforce mgmt that OpenAI API does not provide
research_pointers: Check if NICE moved on the headline
Compare to prior OpenAI Realtime API launch reactions

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Five9 has been incorporating OpenAI tech itself; voice AI commoditization could pressure per-seat economics but expand TAM via voice agents
research_pointers: FIVN has been weak on AI-displacement narrative for months

ai_announcements.headline: Anthropic and OpenAI announce new joint ventures targeting enterprise AI deployment
ai_announcements.summary: Mentioned in TechCrunch podcast roundup; lacks specific details on the JVs.

ai_announcements.headline: Perplexity's Personal Computer agent now broadly available on Mac
ai_announcements.summary: Perplexity's agentic Mac app opens to all users.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Search query share erosion narrative is well-worn; Mac availability of a Perplexity agent is incremental, not category-defining
research_pointers: Track Perplexity MAU disclosures, default-search deals on macOS

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Quiet tape. No stocks shown as moving in market_data. Headlines are mostly incremental (OpenAI voice API, Perplexity Mac launch) or vague (Anthropic/OpenAI JV mention in a podcast blurb without specifics). No high-conviction overdone/underdone calls available.
